element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic ranged attack; shoots arrows that explode enemies
Direction follows on-screen enemies; requires reading enemy position
Shoots criss-cross pattern; demands tactical positioning
Three darts in separate directions; highest complexity projectile
A-button + down; works as movement and attack against some enemies
Eight capsules = temporary invincibility; resource collection mechanic
Introductory vertical platforming with basic enemies
Features Master C.P.U. boss; laser hazards
Water/liquid environment; no boss stage; claustrophobic design
Final stage; two Joker boss battles; no sub-levels
First boss encounter; teaches boss pattern recognition

typical_relief_triggers: Collecting weapon upgrade icons (C, B, N, S)
Safe platforming sections between enemy clusters
typical_tension_triggers: Boss encounters (numbered health meter switching from increments)
Hazard environments (snow mountain, underground water, refinery lasers)
Limited ammunition types requiring weapon switching
Health depletion without nearby capsule pickups
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating intensity across seven stages with two sub-levels per stage (except final); boss battles punctuate each world with numbered health representation creating tonal shift from platforming sections

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gotham Cathedral (Stage 1) introduces basic platforming, slide attacks, and first weapon types; Ace Ranger boss teaches damage avoidance
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stages 3-4 introduce environmental hazards (snow, refinery lasers) and more complex weapon switching requirements; Minedroid and Master C.P.U. bosses demand pattern recognition
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stages 6-7 concentrate enemy density and introduce final Joker battles; Island of Ha-Hacienda features only boss encounters with no sub-level platforming, focusing pure combat challenge
notable_spikes: Stage 3 (Snowy Mountain): introduces environmental hazard type
Stage 4 (Refinery): Master C.P.U. boss with laser arena
Stage 7 (Island of Ha-Hacienda): dual Joker fights with no intermediate platforming relief

notable_level_patterns.description: Six of seven stages contain two sub-levels followed by distinct boss battle; boss fights use numeric health meters (vs. incremental health bars in platforming sections), creating tonal and mechanical clarity
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 1: Cathedral sub-levels → Ace Ranger boss

notable_level_patterns.description: Later stages introduce location-specific hazards: Stage 3 uses snow/ice, Stage 4 uses industrial lasers, Stage 5 uses water; each demands different navigation strategies
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 4 (Refinery) with Master C.P.U. and laser environmental damage

notable_level_patterns.description: Players collect four ammunition types (Crossbow, Batarang, Sonic Neutralizer, Shield Stars) across game; complexity escalates from homing projectiles (Batarang) to multi-directional spray (Shield Stars)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All stages; icon placement incentivizes weapon switching

notable_level_patterns.description: Stage 7 (Island of Ha-Hacienda) removes sub-level platforming entirely, containing only two Joker boss battles; represents shift from exploration to pure combat

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Batman: Return of the Joker (NES)
Wikipedia - Batman: Return of the Joker
